title: Cortex Chess
description: A from-scratch chess engine AND analysis studio in TypeScript — PVS search with transposition tables, SEE, quiescence, null-move + late-move reductions, internal iterative reduction, countermoves and a tapered eval. Endgames are solved exactly in the browser by retrograde analysis: KPK/KRK/KQK plus the full King+Bishop+Knight vs King distance-to-mate table (~33.6M positions, the hardest elementary mate, built and exhaustively self-verified in seconds). A single material-generic retrograde solver derives the whole family of pawnless 3-4-man distance-to-mate tablebases — reproducing the hand-rolled KRK/KQK/KBNvK tables bit-for-bit and newly solving KBBvK (the two-bishop mate), KNNvK and the major-piece combinations via a DTM bucket queue with Syzygy-style sub-table probing; built tables persist to IndexedDB and warm instantly so the engine plays the ending perfectly. Plus UCI-style time management (clock + increment). It even has a from-scratch NNUE (efficiently-updatable neural-network) evaluation — no ML libraries — trained in your browser by knowledge distillation from the hand-crafted eval with a hand-rolled Adam SGD: its first layer is an incrementally-updated accumulator kept bit-for-bit in lock-step with make/unmake (proven against a full refresh), the hand-derived gradients are finite-difference checked, and the NNUE Lab trains it live with a loss curve, a net-vs-classical correlation scatter (R²≈0.92), and an 8-game head-to-head. It even ships a pre-trained default net (R²=0.99, trained offline with the repo's own trainer) so the NNUE eval works on a fresh load with no training — toggle it on to play the engine on a learned eval, or train and save your own to override it. Play with adjustable strength, fixed movetime and real pondering (instant ponder-hits); open the Analyze board to import a PGN, step through with a multi-PV engine, a whole-game evaluation graph, an opening explorer and one-click annotated-PGN export; or hit the Engine Lab to run perft, tactics, the Bratko-Kopec / Win-at-Chess EPD benchmark suites, the tablebase verifier and the NNUE trainer.
